title_en: The brightest tourism trend can be found in the darkest places
title_zh: 最炙手可热的旅游趋势，往往出现在最黑暗的地方
rubric_zh: 观星正在成为一门“发光”生意

paragraphs.en: FOR MILLENNIA starry nights have guided navigators, perplexed astronomers and inspired painters. But they are becoming a rarer sight. At least 80% of the world lives under light-polluted skies—a figure that rises to 99% in America and Europe. A paper published in Science found that between 2011 and 2022 the night sky grew almost 10% brighter each year. With so many places awash with artificial light, people are increasingly seeking out the darkness.
paragraphs.zh: 数千年来，满天繁星的夜空引导过航海者，困惑过天文学家，也激发过画家的灵感。然而，如今它们正日渐稀少。至少有80%的世界人口生活在被光污染笼罩的天空下——在美洲和欧洲，这一比例甚至高达99%。一篇发表于《Science》的论文发现，2011年至2022年间，夜空几乎每年都变亮了10%。在越来越多地区被人工光线淹没的情况下，人们对“黑暗”越来越向往。

paragraphs.en: Many became starry-eyed during the pandemic. Confined to their homes, people took to gazing at the cosmos. (It helped that lockdown contributed to a drop in light pollution in lots of places.) Some turned to social media—and online communities such as “SpaceTok”—for information. Once restrictions lifted, these hobbyists planned their trips accordingly.
paragraphs.zh: 许多人在疫情期间变得“星空情结”十足。被限制在家中，人们开始仰望宇宙。（封控期间不少地区的光污染下降，也起了作用。）一些人转而依赖社交媒体，以及“SpaceTok”等线上社区获取信息。等到限制解除，这些业余爱好者便据此制定了行程。

paragraphs.en: The forecasts for companies offering “astrotourism” and “noctourism” are anything but cloudy. In 2024 a survey by Booking.com, a travel website, found that 62% of people were interested in destinations with limited light pollution. To meet the demand for “cosmic escapism”, some hotels now provide access to telescopes and in-house astronomers. Others are offering star-bathing and moonlit meditation.
paragraphs.zh: 对于提供“天文旅游”（astrotourism）和“夜间旅游”（noctourism）的企业而言，前景一点也不“阴霾”。2024年，一家旅游网站Booking.com的调查显示，62%的人表示对光污染较少的目的地感兴趣。为了满足这种“宇宙式逃离感”需求，一些酒店开始提供望远镜和馆内天文学家服务；另一些则推出了“星空浴”与月光冥想等体验。

paragraphs.en: Because the best stargazing requires seclusion, astrotourism is a boon to remote areas that do not usually lure tourists. DarkSky International, a non-profit, certifies parks and reserves with the best night-time conditions. It has seen a substantial increase in the number of applications in the past three years, growing from 16 in 2023 to 28 applications in the first half of 2026 alone.
paragraphs.zh: 由于最理想的观星需要隔绝喧闹，天文旅游正成为平时难以吸引游客的偏远地区的一大福音。非营利组织DarkSky International会为拥有最佳夜间环境的公园和自然保护区提供认证。该组织过去三年内的申请量显著上升，从2023年的16个增长到2026年前半年单独出现的28个。

paragraphs.en: Astro Retreat in Maan, an Indian village in the Himalayas, has seen a surge in visitors of late. Al Ula, in the Saudi Arabian desert, is going big on astrotourism by building a state-of-the-art observatory and luxury stargazing lodges. John Barentine, an astronomer in Arizona, says there is an opportunity for rural places where industries such as mining have waned.
paragraphs.zh: 位于喜马拉雅山的印度村庄Maan里的Astro Retreat最近吸引了大量游客。沙特阿拉伯沙漠中的Al Ula则正通过建设一座顶尖天文台和豪华观星小屋，全面推动观星旅游。亚利桑那州天文学家John Barentine表示，那些采矿等传统产业已式微的农村地区，正在迎来机会。

paragraphs.en: Such trips appeal because they offer a stillness that is hard to find in much of the modern world, where “we have turned night into day in the places we live and work,” as Ruskin Hartley, DarkSky’s director, puts it. Stargazing also encourages feelings of wonderment, which are in short supply. Studies have found that those who look up report a greater sense of well-being; feelings of awe are linked to lower stress levels and a sense of tranquillity. Some credit the pastime with giving them a greater sense of perspective. As Oscar Wilde once wrote: “We are all in the gutter, but some of us are looking at the stars.” ■
paragraphs.zh: 这类行程之所以吸引人，是因为它们提供了一种在现代社会里难以找到的静谧。正如DarkSky的负责人Ruskin Hartley所说，“我们把居住和工作的地方都照得像白天一样。”观星也能激发对世界的惊奇与敬畏感，而这种情绪近来弥足珍贵。研究发现，经常仰望星空的人报告的幸福感更强；敬畏感与更低的压力水平以及更强的宁静感有关。有些人把这种消遣视作让自己获得更宏大人生视角的方式。正如奥斯卡·王尔德曾写道：“我们都在阴沟里，但有些人正在仰望星空。”■

paragraphs.en: For more on the latest books, films, TV shows, albums and controversies, sign up to Plot Twist, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter
paragraphs.zh: 想获取更多关于最新图书、电影、电视剧、专辑与争议的内容，请订阅Plot Twist，我们每周面向订阅者发布的简报。
